## Required reading

Read every file below end-to-end before writing a single line of code.

- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/workpackage-tracker-v3.md` — §6 T3 MRK-V3-03 entry (line 1507); §3 Design Pillars D1–D10 + anti-patterns A1–A10 (line 73); §A Agent Instructions (line 3842)
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/CLAUDE.md`
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/comments.py` — existing `Comment` entity + `CommentAnchor` union; MRK-V3-03 extends this with a `SheetAnchor`
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/tests/api/test_comments_route.py` — existing comment route tests; extend here
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/workspace/SheetCanvas.tsx` — sheet renderer; needs source-element binding hooks for the new anchor
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/collab/CommentPin.tsx` — pin renderer for plan + 3D; extend to sheet surface
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/workspace/CommentsPanel.tsx` — existing comment panel; reuse for sheet-side thread popover
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/core/src/index.ts` — all element types; add `SheetAnchor` here
- _Source:_ R-B §9 #1 — Motif's sheet review with bidirectional comment back-flow; the email/PDF chain replacement WP.

---

## Verbatim MRK-V3-03 tracker entry (§6 T3, line 1507)

#### MRK-V3-03 — Sheet review surface with comment-anchoring

_Source: R-B §9 #1 (sheet review with bidirectional Revit comments — the email/PDF chain practices universally hate). Severity High. The Bluebeam-replacement WP._

**Status.** `next`.
**Scope.** Published sheets render in a dedicated review surface inside the canvas. Reviewers (including public-link viewers) can drop comments + markups on sheet detail callouts; the comment back-flows to the source view from which the detail was sliced. Comment chip appears in the source view's chrome: "1 comment on detail B-12 from sheet A-301." Click → jumps to source view with the comment surfaced as a pin. Removes the email/PDF chain that practices universally hate.
**Data model.** Reuses MRK-V3-01 `Comment` entity with extended anchor:
```ts
anchor:
  | …
  | { kind: 'sheet'; sheetId: string; xPx: number; yPx: number; sourceViewId?: string; sourceElementId?: string };
```
**Engine.** Sheet anchor includes optional `sourceViewId` + `sourceElementId` populated by the sheet renderer (which knows which view and element each pixel maps from). When the comment is created with a sheet anchor, server resolves the source binding and surfaces a notification chip on the source view. Bidirectional: closing the comment in either surface (sheet or source view) closes both.
**UI.** Sheet review surface is a dedicated full-screen surface (not a modal) — sheet renders at print fidelity, review toolbar at the bottom (`CM` comment, `AN` markup, `MR` resolve). Source-view chip: small flag glyph in the view header with a count; click opens a panel listing sheet-originated comments.
**Acceptance.** Publish a sheet with detail B-12 sliced from plan view P-1. Reviewer opens the public link, drops a comment on detail B-12. Architect, working in plan view P-1, sees a chip "1 comment from sheet A-301" within 2s. Click → comment surfaces as a pin on the actual element in P-1. Resolve from P-1; the sheet-side comment also resolves.
**Effort.** L — 2.5 weeks (depends on T4's sheet rendering being mature enough to expose source-element bindings).
**Cross-theme references.** T4 (sheets + source-element bindings); MRK-V3-01 (Comment entity); T9 (reviewer-suggested edits on sheets can be picked up by the agent as a refinement prompt).

---

## Setup

```bash
cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
git fetch origin
git checkout main
git pull --ff-only
git checkout -b feat/v3-mrk-v3-03-sheet-review
python -c "from bim_ai.comments import Comment; print('comments ok')"
uv sync && pnpm install
```

---

## Implementation tasks

### 1. Extend `SheetAnchor` in Python (`app/bim_ai/comments.py`)

Add a new anchor variant alongside `ElementAnchor`, `PointAnchor`, `RegionAnchor`:

```python
class SheetAnchor(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    kind: Literal["sheet"] = "sheet"
    sheet_id: str = Field(alias="sheetId")
    x_px: float = Field(alias="xPx")
    y_px: float = Field(alias="yPx")
    source_view_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="sourceViewId")
    source_element_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="sourceElementId")
```

Extend the `CommentAnchor` discriminated union to include `SheetAnchor`:

```python
CommentAnchor = Annotated[
    ElementAnchor | PointAnchor | RegionAnchor | SheetAnchor,
    Field(discriminator="kind"),
]
```

No other changes to `Comment` — the entity is unchanged; only the anchor union grows.

### 2. Source-binding resolution (`app/bim_ai/comments.py`)

Add a helper called at comment-creation time when the anchor kind is `"sheet"`:

```python
def resolve_sheet_anchor_binding(
    anchor: SheetAnchor,
    sheet_pixel_map: dict[tuple[int, int], tuple[str, str]],
    # maps (x_px_rounded, y_px_rounded) → (sourceViewId, sourceElementId)
) -> SheetAnchor:
    """Populate sourceViewId + sourceElementId from the sheet pixel map if available."""
    key = (round(anchor.x_px), round(anchor.y_px))
    binding = sheet_pixel_map.get(key)
    if binding:
        return anchor.model_copy(update={"source_view_id": binding[0], "source_element_id": binding[1]})
    return anchor
```

The pixel map is provided by the sheet renderer at request time. For v3, the map is returned as a JSON sidecar by `GET /api/v3/sheets/{sheetId}/pixel-map` (see task 3). If no map entry exists for the clicked pixel, the anchor stores `None` for both optional fields — the comment is still valid, just without back-flow.

### 3. New REST endpoint: pixel map (`app/bim_ai/routes_api.py`)

```
GET /api/v3/models/{modelId}/sheets/{sheetId}/pixel-map
```

Returns a JSON object `{ "map": { "<x>,<y>": { "sourceViewId": "…", "sourceElementId": "…" } } }` for integer pixel coordinates. The server-side sheet renderer populates this by walking `sheet.viewPlacements`, converting each placement's bounding box to pixel ranges, and recording the viewId + element under each pixel from the source view's hit-test index. For v3, approximate coverage is sufficient — all pixels in a view-placement bounding box map to that viewId; sourceElementId is populated only where a hit-test index exists.

Register the endpoint under the existing `router` in `routes_api.py`. Requires `viewer` or above permission (public-link viewers included).

### 4. Notification chip on source view (`app/bim_ai/routes_api.py` + `app/bim_ai/activity.py`)

When a `POST /api/v3/models/{modelId}/comments` lands with `anchor.kind == "sheet"` and `anchor.sourceViewId` is not `None`:

- Write a notification entry to the activity stream for the source view: `{ kind: "sheet_comment_chip", viewId: sourceViewId, sheetId: sheetId, commentId: commentId, sheetNumber: … }`.
- The activity stream already broadcasts via WebSocket; the chip appears within 2 s for connected clients (CQ-01 prerequisite — assumed done).

Add `SheetCommentChipEntry` to `activity.py` alongside existing activity entry types.

### 5. Bidirectional resolve (`app/bim_ai/routes_api.py`)

When a comment with a `sheet` anchor is resolved (via `POST /api/v3/models/{modelId}/comments/{commentId}/resolve`), also mark resolved any comment in the same `threadId` that has a matching `sourceElementId` anchor on the source view (element anchor pointing at `sourceElementId`). Symmetric: resolving from the element-anchor side resolves the sheet-side thread.

Implement as a post-resolve hook in the existing resolve handler. This is a best-effort match — if no matching thread exists on the element side, resolve proceeds silently.

### 6. TypeScript anchor type (`packages/core/src/index.ts`)

Add `SheetAnchor` to the `CommentAnchor` discriminated union alongside existing anchor types:

```ts
export type SheetAnchor = {
  kind: 'sheet';
  sheetId: string;
  xPx: number;
  yPx: number;
  sourceViewId?: string;
  sourceElementId?: string;
};
```

Extend `CommentAnchor = ElementAnchor | PointAnchor | RegionAnchor | SheetAnchor`.

### 7. `SheetReviewSurface` component (`packages/web/src/plan/SheetReviewSurface.tsx`)

Full-screen review surface (not a modal — anti-pattern A4 antidote):

```tsx
type SheetReviewSurfaceProps = {
  sheetId: string;
  modelId: string;
  readOnly?: boolean; // true for public-link viewers without editor role
};
```

- Renders `<SheetCanvas>` at print fidelity occupying the full viewport minus the 40 px bottom toolbar.
- Bottom review toolbar (40 px, `--color-surface-strong`, `--radius-canvas: 0`): three shortcut chips — `CM` comment, `AN` markup, `MR` resolve — matching the existing toolbar pattern from `MarkupToolbar.tsx`.
- In `CM` mode, a click on the sheet canvas: (a) fetches the pixel map (cached per sheetId, 30 s TTL); (b) resolves `sourceViewId` + `sourceElementId`; (c) opens a `CommentsPanel` thread popover at `--space-2` from the pin; (d) on post, dispatches `POST /comments` with a `sheet` anchor.
- Comment pins on the sheet render as `<CommentPin>` with `kind="sheet"` variant — same SDF glyph at constant screen size, no hex literals.
- Uses `var(--color-surface-1)` for the sheet background pass-through.

### 8. `SourceViewChip` component (`packages/web/src/workspace/SourceViewChip.tsx`)

Small chip in the view header (plan view, 3D view) surfacing sheet-originated comments:

```tsx
type SourceViewChipProps = {
  viewId: string;
  modelId: string;
};
```

- Reads from the activity stream (WebSocket) for `sheet_comment_chip` entries matching `viewId`.
- Renders a flag glyph (`var(--draft-comment-pin)` weight, 16 × 16 px) with a badge count when count > 0.
- On click: opens a panel (slides in from right, 300 px, 200 ms `--ease-paper`) listing sheet-originated comment threads. Each row: sheet number + comment excerpt. Click a row → navigates to the `SheetReviewSurface` for that sheet with the comment pin highlighted.
- Zero state: chip is hidden (`display: none`) — D1 / A9 antidote.
- No hex literals. Tokens only.

### 9. Wire `SourceViewChip` into view headers

In `packages/web/src/workspace/TopBar.tsx` (or whichever component renders the active view's header bar), import and render `<SourceViewChip viewId={activeViewId} modelId={modelId} />` alongside existing view-header slots. Hidden until a sheet comment arrives.

### 10. Tests (`app/tests/api/test_mrk_v3_03.py`)

Cover:

- `POST /comments` with `sheet` anchor stores a `SheetAnchor` — kind, sheetId, xPx, yPx serialise correctly.
- `POST /comments` with sheet anchor + valid pixel-map binding populates `sourceViewId` + `sourceElementId`.
- `POST /comments` with sheet anchor + no pixel-map entry stores `sourceViewId=None`.
- Activity stream receives a `sheet_comment_chip` entry with correct `viewId` and `sheetId` after post.
- Resolving a sheet-anchored comment from the sheet side also resolves a thread-matched element-anchored comment on the source element.
- Resolving from the element-anchor side also resolves the sheet-side thread.
- `GET /sheets/{sheetId}/pixel-map` returns 200 with a `map` key; requires at least viewer permission.
- Unauthenticated request to pixel-map returns 401.
- `mark_orphaned_comments` still works unchanged for `element`, `point`, `region` anchors alongside the new `sheet` anchor.

---

## Implementation rules

- **Agent-callable contract per API-V3-01.** Every new route must have: HTTP endpoint registered in `routes_api.py`, Pydantic request/response schemas, and a JSON schema entry in `app/bim_ai/commands.py` if it mutates state. CLI exposure is not required for this WP (the sheet review surface is UI-driven), but the REST contract must be stable.
- **No scope creep.** Do not author BCF export, IFC bindings, or any T9 agent tooling in this WP. Do not ship live-cursor overlays on the sheet surface (COL-V3-04, `vision`). Do not add a chat sidebar.
- **Design-pillar adherence.** Sheet review surface is full-screen, never a modal (A4 antidote). Bottom toolbar shortcut chips carry text labels at widths ≥ 240 px (A5 antidote). `SourceViewChip` is hidden when count is zero (A9 antidote). Resolve from either surface; no separate "synch" step (A6 antidote). D6 load-bearing: comment threads are first-class, threadable, resolvable, non-modal.
- **No hex literals in `packages/web/src/**/*.tsx`.** The ESLint rule (`bim-ai/no-hex-in-chrome`) will fail CI. Use `var(--color-*)`, `var(--draft-*)`, `var(--disc-*)` exclusively. If a token is missing, add it to `tokens-v3.css` — do not hardcode values.

---

```bash
SheetAnchor extends Comment entity; pixel-map endpoint resolves source-view
binding; bidirectional resolve across sheet + element anchors; SheetReviewSurface

surfaces back-flow count with flag glyph.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
```

**Branch:** `feat/v3-ann-v3-01-detail-region`
**Effort:** M — 1 week
**Status:** `now` (deps met: v2 ANN-01 partial schema + EDT-01 done)

---

## Required reading

Read every file below end-to-end before writing a single line of code.

- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/workpackage-tracker-v3.md` — §6 T4 ANN-V3-01 entry; §3 Design Pillars D1, D3, D8; §A Agent Instructions
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/CLAUDE.md`
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/commands.py` — existing `CreateDetailRegionCmd` / `UpdateDetailRegionCmd` near bottom; v2 partial impl
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/engine.py` — `apply_inplace` dispatch table; where to add the new handler
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/elements.py` — `DetailRegionElem` if present; otherwise add it
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/core/src/index.ts` — all element types; add `DetailRegionElem` + `DraftDetailRegionElem` here
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/plan/` — plan-view tool infrastructure; where to add `DetailRegionTool`
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/api/registry.py` — add `draw-detail-region` tool descriptor

---

## Verbatim ANN-V3-01 tracker entry

#### ANN-V3-01 — Detail-region drawing-mode authoring (ANN-01 carry-forward)

**Status.** `now`. v2 ANN-01 deferred drawing-mode UX; setter + chip exist.

**Scope.** Click-to-author polyline / region tool that drops a `detail_region` element on a plan, section, drafting view, or callout. Polyline (open path) or region (closed path with hatch). Live preview, escape cancels, return commits.

**Engine + UI.** v2 partial schema `{ kind: 'detail_region'; viewId; vertices; closed; hatchId?; lineweightOverride?; phase_* }`. `CreateDetailRegion` / `UpdateDetailRegion` exist; this WP adds a `DraftDetailRegion` transient hosting live-preview vertices. Tool palette → "Detail Region", shortcut `DR`. Soft contextual scope per SKT-01. Polyline default; tap `R` mid-command to switch to region. Hatch picker in OptionsBar (CAN-V3-02 library).

**Acceptance.** In a callout view, draw a 5-segment closed region; assign brick hatch; commit; renders at the callout's plot scale with paper-mm-correct hatch density. Phase-aware.

**Effort.** M — 1 week.

---

## Setup

```bash
cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
git fetch origin
git checkout main
git pull --ff-only
git checkout -b feat/v3-ann-v3-01-detail-region
uv sync && pnpm install
python -c "from bim_ai.commands import CreateDetailRegionCmd; print('cmd ok')" 2>/dev/null || echo "cmd missing — add it"
```

---

## Implementation tasks

### 1. `DetailRegionElem` in Python (`app/bim_ai/elements.py`)

Ensure `DetailRegionElem` exists with the full schema. If present, audit it; if missing, add:

```python
class DetailRegionElem(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    kind: Literal["detail_region"] = "detail_region"
    id: str
    view_id: str = Field(alias="viewId")
    vertices: list[dict]          # list of {x: float, y: float} in mm
    closed: bool = False
    hatch_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="hatchId")
    lineweight_override: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="lineweightOverride")
    phase_created: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseCreated")
    phase_demolished: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseDemolished")
```

### 2. Commands (`app/bim_ai/commands.py`)

Ensure `CreateDetailRegionCmd` and `UpdateDetailRegionCmd` are in the `Command` union. Add if missing:

```python
class CreateDetailRegionCmd(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["create_detail_region"] = "create_detail_region"
    id: str
    view_id: str = Field(alias="viewId")
    vertices: list[dict]          # [{x, y}] in mm
    closed: bool = False
    hatch_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="hatchId")
    lineweight_override: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="lineweightOverride")
    phase_created: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseCreated")

class UpdateDetailRegionCmd(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["update_detail_region"] = "update_detail_region"
    id: str
    vertices: list[dict] | None = None
    closed: bool | None = None
    hatch_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="hatchId")
    lineweight_override: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="lineweightOverride")
    phase_demolished: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseDemolished")
```

Both must appear in `Command = Annotated[... | CreateDetailRegionCmd | UpdateDetailRegionCmd, Field(discriminator="type")]`.

### 3. Engine handlers (`app/bim_ai/engine.py`)

In `apply_inplace`, add cases:

```python
case CreateDetailRegionCmd():
    elem = DetailRegionElem(
        id=cmd.id,
        viewId=cmd.view_id,
        vertices=cmd.vertices,
        closed=cmd.closed,
        hatchId=cmd.hatch_id,
        lineweightOverride=cmd.lineweight_override,
        phaseCreated=cmd.phase_created,
    )
    model.elements[cmd.id] = elem

case UpdateDetailRegionCmd():
    existing = model.elements.get(cmd.id)
    if existing is None or existing.kind != "detail_region":
        raise ValueError(f"No detail_region element with id {cmd.id}")
    patch = cmd.model_dump(exclude_unset=True, by_alias=True)
    patch.pop("type", None)
    updated = existing.model_copy(update=patch)
    model.elements[cmd.id] = updated
```

### 4. TypeScript types (`packages/core/src/index.ts`)

Add to the `BimElem` union and export:

```ts
export type DetailRegionElem = {
  kind: 'detail_region';
  id: string;
  viewId: string;
  vertices: Array<{ x: number; y: number }>;
  closed: boolean;
  hatchId?: string | null;
  lineweightOverride?: number | null;
  phaseCreated?: string | null;
  phaseDemolished?: string | null;
};

/** Transient: live-preview vertices before commit. Never persisted. */
export type DraftDetailRegionElem = {
  kind: 'draft_detail_region';
  viewId: string;
  vertices: Array<{ x: number; y: number }>;
  closed: boolean;
  hatchId?: string | null;
};
```

Add `'detail_region'` to `ElemKind` union. Add `DetailRegionElem` to `BimElem` union.

### 5. Tool registry descriptor (`app/bim_ai/api/registry.py`)

Add a `draw-detail-region` descriptor in the annotation tools section:

```python
ToolDescriptor(
    name="draw-detail-region",
    description="Draw a polyline or closed region on a plan/section/drafting/callout view. Polyline = annotation path; closed = hatch region. Returns the created element id.",
    input_schema={
        "type": "object",
        "properties": {
            "model_id": {"type": "string"},
            "view_id": {"type": "string", "description": "Id of the target view"},
            "vertices": {
                "type": "array",
                "items": {"type": "object", "properties": {"x": {"type": "number"}, "y": {"type": "number"}}, "required": ["x", "y"]},
                "description": "Vertex list in mm relative to plan origin"
            },
            "closed": {"type": "boolean", "default": False},
            "hatch_id": {"type": "string", "description": "Hatch pattern id; only used when closed=true"},
            "lineweight_override": {"type": "number", "description": "Override line weight in mm; null = category default"},
        },
        "required": ["model_id", "view_id", "vertices"],
    },
),
```

### 6. CLI exposure (`packages/cli/cli.mjs`)

Add a `detail-region` command following the existing command pattern:

```js
if (cmd === 'detail-region') {
  // ANN-V3-01: draw a detail region polyline or closed hatch region on a view
  const [modelId, viewId, ...rest] = args;
  const vertices = JSON.parse(rest[0] || '[]');
  const closed = rest[1] === '--closed';
  const hatchId = rest.find(a => a.startsWith('--hatch='))?.split('=')[1] ?? null;
  const data = await apiFetch(`/api/v3/models/${modelId}/apply`, {
    method: 'POST',
    body: JSON.stringify({
      commands: [{
        type: 'create_detail_region',
        id: crypto.randomUUID(),
        viewId,
        vertices,
        closed,
        hatchId,
      }],
    }),
  });
  console.log(JSON.stringify(data, null, 2));
}
```

### 7. Frontend tool (`packages/web/src/plan/DetailRegionTool.tsx`)

New component: polyline drawing tool for plan/2D views.

- State: `vertices: XY[]`, `closed: boolean`, `hatchId: string | null`
- On canvas click: push vertex; render a `DraftDetailRegionElem` (transient) connecting all vertices with the active line weight
- `R` key mid-command: toggle `closed` (when closed, fills with hatch preview at 30 % opacity using `var(--draft-witness)`)
- `Enter` / double-click last vertex: commit → dispatch `CreateDetailRegionCmd`
- `Esc`: cancel, clear transient
- Shortcut `DR` to activate (two-key sequence; chord within 400 ms)
- OptionsBar slot (when tool is active): hatch picker chip. For v3, seed 3 hatches: `brick_45`, `concrete_dot`, `insulation_curve`. No hex literals — tokens only.

### 8. Detail-region renderer (`packages/web/src/plan/DetailRegionRenderer.tsx`)

For committed `detail_region` elements in a view:

- Polyline (closed=false): stroke with `--draft-lw-medium`, `var(--color-foreground)`
- Closed region: stroke + hatch fill. For now, render hatch as repeating SVG `<line>` pattern at 45° spaced `73 / (view.scale / 100)` screen px. CAN-V3-02 will replace this with the full hatch engine.
- Phase-demolished: render dashed in `var(--draft-witness)` at 50 % opacity.

Register as a layer in the plan view canvas renderer (alongside existing wall/door/window layers).

### 9. Tests (`app/tests/api/test_ann_v3_01.py`)

Cover:

- `CreateDetailRegionCmd` stores a `DetailRegionElem` with correct vertices/closed/hatchId.
- `UpdateDetailRegionCmd` patches vertices and closed flag.
- Engine raises `ValueError` if updating a non-existent or wrong-kind element.
- Draw-detail-region tool descriptor is present in `/api/v3/tools`.
- Phase-demolished element is returned in snapshots filtered to the demolition phase.
- Round-trip: create → fetch snapshot → verify element present with correct kind + viewId.

---

## Implementation rules

- **API-V3-01 contract:** new commands must appear in the `Command` union, have CLI exposure, and be accessible via REST.
- **No scope creep.** Do not implement ANN-V3-02 (detail-component library). Do not implement CAN-V3-02 hatch engine — simple SVG stub is fine.
- **Design pillar D1.** Drawing happens on the canvas (click-to-draw), not in a dialog. OptionsBar for hatch selection is inline.
- **No hex literals** in any `packages/web/src/**/*.tsx`. Tokens only.

---

## Verify gate

```bash
cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
make verify
pnpm exec tsc --noEmit
cd app && python -m pytest tests/api/test_ann_v3_01.py -v && python -m pytest tests/ -x -q
```

All gates green before committing.

---

## Commit and push

```bash
        app/bim_ai/elements.py \
        app/bim_ai/engine.py \
        app/bim_ai/api/registry.py \

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'

CreateDetailRegionCmd/UpdateDetailRegionCmd + engine handlers; DraftDetailRegion
transient for live preview; polyline + closed-region tool with hatch stub;
DR shortcut + OptionsBar hatch picker; draw-detail-region tool descriptor.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
git push -u origin feat/v3-ann-v3-01-detail-region
```

```python
    hatch_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="hatchId")
    lineweight_override: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="lineweightOverride")
    phase_created: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseCreated")
    phase_demolished: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseDemolished")
```

### 2. Commands (`app/bim_ai/commands.py`)

Ensure `CreateDetailRegionCmd` and `UpdateDetailRegionCmd` are in the `Command` union. Add if missing:

```python
class CreateDetailRegionCmd(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["create_detail_region"] = "create_detail_region"
    id: str
    view_id: str = Field(alias="viewId")
    vertices: list[dict]          # [{x, y}] in mm
    closed: bool = False
    hatch_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="hatchId")
    lineweight_override: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="lineweightOverride")
    phase_created: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseCreated")

class UpdateDetailRegionCmd(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["update_detail_region"] = "update_detail_region"
    id: str
    vertices: list[dict] | None = None
    closed: bool | None = None
    hatch_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="hatchId")
    lineweight_override: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="lineweightOverride")
    phase_demolished: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseDemolished")
```

Both must appear in `Command = Annotated[... | CreateDetailRegionCmd | UpdateDetailRegionCmd, Field(discriminator="type")]`.

### 3. Engine handlers (`app/bim_ai/engine.py`)

In `apply_inplace`, add cases:

```python
case CreateDetailRegionCmd():
    elem = DetailRegionElem(
        id=cmd.id,
        viewId=cmd.view_id,
        vertices=cmd.vertices,
        closed=cmd.closed,
        hatchId=cmd.hatch_id,
        lineweightOverride=cmd.lineweight_override,
        phaseCreated=cmd.phase_created,
    )
    model.elements[cmd.id] = elem

case UpdateDetailRegionCmd():
    existing = model.elements.get(cmd.id)
    if existing is None or existing.kind != "detail_region":
        raise ValueError(f"No detail_region element with id {cmd.id}")
    patch = cmd.model_dump(exclude_unset=True, by_alias=True)
    patch.pop("type", None)
    updated = existing.model_copy(update=patch)
    model.elements[cmd.id] = updated
```

### 4. TypeScript types (`packages/core/src/index.ts`)

Add to the `BimElem` union and export:

```ts
export type DetailRegionElem = {
  kind: 'detail_region';
  id: string;
  viewId: string;
  vertices: Array<{ x: number; y: number }>;
  closed: boolean;
  hatchId?: string | null;
  lineweightOverride?: number | null;
  phaseCreated?: string | null;
  phaseDemolished?: string | null;
};

/** Transient: live-preview vertices before commit. Never persisted. */
export type DraftDetailRegionElem = {
  kind: 'draft_detail_region';
  viewId: string;
  vertices: Array<{ x: number; y: number }>;
  closed: boolean;
  hatchId?: string | null;
};
```

Add `'detail_region'` to `ElemKind` union. Add `DetailRegionElem` to `BimElem` union.

### 5. Tool registry descriptor (`app/bim_ai/api/registry.py`)

Add a `draw-detail-region` descriptor in the annotation tools section:

```python
ToolDescriptor(
    name="draw-detail-region",
    description="Draw a polyline or closed region on a plan/section/drafting/callout view. Polyline = annotation path; closed = hatch region. Returns the created element id.",
    input_schema={
        "type": "object",
        "properties": {
            "model_id": {"type": "string"},
            "view_id": {"type": "string", "description": "Id of the target view"},
            "vertices": {
                "type": "array",
                "items": {"type": "object", "properties": {"x": {"type": "number"}, "y": {"type": "number"}}, "required": ["x", "y"]},
                "description": "Vertex list in mm relative to plan origin"
            },
            "closed": {"type": "boolean", "default": False},
            "hatch_id": {"type": "string", "description": "Hatch pattern id; only used when closed=true"},
            "lineweight_override": {"type": "number", "description": "Override line weight in mm; null = category default"},
        },
        "required": ["model_id", "view_id", "vertices"],
    },
),
```

### 6. CLI exposure (`packages/cli/cli.mjs`)

Add a `detail-region` command following the existing command pattern:

```js
if (cmd === 'detail-region') {
  // ANN-V3-01: draw a detail region polyline or closed hatch region on a view
  const [modelId, viewId, ...rest] = args;
  const vertices = JSON.parse(rest[0] || '[]');
  const closed = rest[1] === '--closed';
  const hatchId = rest.find(a => a.startsWith('--hatch='))?.split('=')[1] ?? null;
  const data = await apiFetch(`/api/v3/models/${modelId}/apply`, {
    method: 'POST',
    body: JSON.stringify({
      commands: [{
        type: 'create_detail_region',
        id: crypto.randomUUID(),
        viewId,
        vertices,
        closed,
        hatchId,
      }],
    }),
  });
  console.log(JSON.stringify(data, null, 2));
}
```

### 7. Frontend tool (`packages/web/src/plan/DetailRegionTool.tsx`)

New component: polyline drawing tool for plan/2D views.

- State: `vertices: XY[]`, `closed: boolean`, `hatchId: string | null`
- On canvas click: push vertex; render a `DraftDetailRegionElem` (transient) connecting all vertices with the active line weight
- `R` key mid-command: toggle `closed` (when closed, fills with hatch preview at 30 % opacity using `var(--draft-witness)`)
- `Enter` / double-click last vertex: commit → dispatch `CreateDetailRegionCmd`
- `Esc`: cancel, clear transient
- Shortcut `DR` to activate (two-key sequence; chord within 400 ms)
- OptionsBar slot (when tool is active): hatch picker chip. For v3, seed 3 hatches: `brick_45`, `concrete_dot`, `insulation_curve`. No hex literals — tokens only.

### 8. Detail-region renderer (`packages/web/src/plan/DetailRegionRenderer.tsx`)

For committed `detail_region` elements in a view:

- Polyline (closed=false): stroke with `--draft-lw-medium`, `var(--color-foreground)`
- Closed region: stroke + hatch fill. For now, render hatch as repeating SVG `<line>` pattern at 45° spaced `73 / (view.scale / 100)` screen px. CAN-V3-02 will replace this with the full hatch engine.
- Phase-demolished: render dashed in `var(--draft-witness)` at 50 % opacity.

Register as a layer in the plan view canvas renderer (alongside existing wall/door/window layers).

### 9. Tests (`app/tests/api/test_ann_v3_01.py`)

Cover:

- `CreateDetailRegionCmd` stores a `DetailRegionElem` with correct vertices/closed/hatchId.
- `UpdateDetailRegionCmd` patches vertices and closed flag.
- Engine raises `ValueError` if updating a non-existent or wrong-kind element.
- Draw-detail-region tool descriptor is present in `/api/v3/tools`.
- Phase-demolished element is returned in snapshots filtered to the demolition phase.
- Round-trip: create → fetch snapshot → verify element present with correct kind + viewId.

---

## Implementation rules

- **API-V3-01 contract:** new commands must appear in the `Command` union, have CLI exposure, and be accessible via REST.
- **No scope creep.** Do not implement ANN-V3-02 (detail-component library). Do not implement CAN-V3-02 hatch engine — simple SVG stub is fine.
- **Design pillar D1.** Drawing happens on the canvas (click-to-draw), not in a dialog. OptionsBar for hatch selection is inline.
- **No hex literals** in any `packages/web/src/**/*.tsx`. Tokens only.


All gates green before committing.

```bash
CreateDetailRegionCmd/UpdateDetailRegionCmd + engine handlers; DraftDetailRegion
transient for live preview; polyline + closed-region tool with hatch stub;

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

```

# WP-049 — DSC-V3-02: Discipline tags on views (defaultLens + SetViewLens)

**Branch:** `feat/v3-dsc-v3-02-view-discipline-tags`
**Effort:** M — 1.5 weeks
**Status:** `now` (depends on DSC-V3-01 — now `done`)

---

## Required reading

Read every file below end-to-end before writing a single line of code.

- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/workpackage-tracker-v3.md` — §8 T8 DSC-V3-02 + LNS-V3-01 entries; §3 Design Pillars D4, D8; anti-pattern A8; §A Agent Instructions
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/CLAUDE.md`
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/commands.py` — existing command union; add `SetViewLensCmd` here
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/engine.py` — `apply_inplace`; add lens handler
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/elements.py` — view element types; add `defaultLens` field
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/core/src/index.ts` — `ViewElem` (or equivalent); add `LensMode` + `defaultLens`
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/Viewport.tsx` — 3D renderer; add ghost/foreground pass
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/plan/PlanRenderer.tsx` (or equivalent) — 2D plan renderer; add ghost pass

---

## Verbatim DSC-V3-02 tracker entry

#### DSC-V3-02 — Discipline tags on views

**Status.** `now`.
**Scope.** Every view gains a `defaultLens: LensMode`. Values: `'show_arch'`, `'show_struct'`, `'show_mep'`, `'show_all'`. Default at view-create inherits from active workspace's `defaultLens`. The lens is a view modifier — does not mutate the model; changes which elements are foreground vs ghost.
**Data model.**
```ts
type LensMode = 'show_arch' | 'show_struct' | 'show_mep' | 'show_all';
defaultLens: LensMode;  // view-level; default = 'show_all'
```
**Engine.** Snapshot resolution: element matches lens (or lens is `show_all`) → foreground; otherwise ghost at 25 % opacity. `'show_all'` puts everything full opacity. Hidden categories override the lens (separate axis).
**Acceptance.** Create a view → `defaultLens = 'show_struct'`. Arch walls render 25 % ghost; structural elements stay foreground. Switching workspace doesn't mutate views.
**Effort.** M — 1.5 weeks.

---

## Setup

```bash
cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
git fetch origin
git checkout main
git pull --ff-only
git checkout -b feat/v3-dsc-v3-02-view-discipline-tags
uv sync && pnpm install
python -c "from bim_ai.commands import SetViewLensCmd; print('ok')" 2>/dev/null || echo "cmd missing — add it"
```

---

## Implementation tasks

### 1. `LensMode` type + `SetViewLensCmd` (`app/bim_ai/commands.py`)

```python
from typing import Literal
LensMode = Literal["show_arch", "show_struct", "show_mep", "show_all"]

class SetViewLensCmd(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["set_view_lens"] = "set_view_lens"
    view_id: str = Field(alias="viewId")
    lens: LensMode
```

Add `SetViewLensCmd` to the `Command` union.

### 2. `defaultLens` on view elements (`app/bim_ai/elements.py`)

All view element classes (FloorPlanView, SectionView, ElevationView, DraftingView, CalloutView, View3dElem — whatever exists) gain:

```python
default_lens: LensMode = Field(default="show_all", alias="defaultLens")
```

If views are stored in a generic dict rather than typed classes, ensure `defaultLens` defaults to `"show_all"` in the snapshot serialisation.

### 3. Engine handler (`app/bim_ai/engine.py`)

In `apply_inplace`:

```python
case SetViewLensCmd():
    view = model.elements.get(cmd.view_id)
    if view is None:
        raise ValueError(f"View not found: {cmd.view_id}")
    model.elements[cmd.view_id] = view.model_copy(update={"default_lens": cmd.lens})
```

Also implement a helper used by the rendering layer:

```python
def element_passes_lens(elem_discipline: str | None, lens: str) -> bool:
    """Return True if the element should be foreground under the given lens."""
    if lens == "show_all":
        return True
    mapping = {"show_arch": "arch", "show_struct": "struct", "show_mep": "mep"}
    return elem_discipline == mapping.get(lens)
```

### 4. TypeScript types (`packages/core/src/index.ts`)

```ts
export type LensMode = 'show_arch' | 'show_struct' | 'show_mep' | 'show_all';
```

Add `defaultLens?: LensMode` to every view element type (`ViewElem`, `View`, `DraftingView`, etc.). Default `'show_all'`.

Add `SetViewLensCmd` to the command union:

```ts
export type SetViewLensCmd = { type: 'set_view_lens'; viewId: string; lens: LensMode };
```

### 5. CLI exposure (`packages/cli/cli.mjs`)

```js
if (cmd === 'set-view-lens') {
  // DSC-V3-02: set discipline lens on a view
  const [modelId, viewId, lens] = args;
  const data = await apiFetch(`/api/v3/models/${modelId}/apply`, {
    method: 'POST',
    body: JSON.stringify({ commands: [{ type: 'set_view_lens', viewId, lens }] }),
  });
  console.log(JSON.stringify(data, null, 2));
}
```

### 6. Ghost/foreground rendering pass (frontend)

**In the plan renderer** (`packages/web/src/plan/` — locate the canvas render loop):

When rendering plan elements, check the active view's `defaultLens`. Elements whose `discipline` does not match the lens render at `opacity: 0.25` using `var(--draft-witness)` as their stroke colour. `show_all` → all foreground. Elements with `discipline: null` are treated as `arch`.

**In the 3D viewport** (`packages/web/src/Viewport.tsx` or `meshBuilders.ts`):

Apply the same opacity pass: ghosted meshes render at 25 % opacity with `--draft-witness` material override. This is a CSS/WebGL uniform override, not a structural mesh change.

Implement `useLensFilter(viewId)` hook returning `(elem: BimElem) => 'foreground' | 'ghost'`. Used by both renderers.

### 7. Tool descriptor (`app/bim_ai/api/registry.py`)

```python
ToolDescriptor(
    name="set-view-lens",
    description="Set the discipline lens on a view. Elements not matching the lens render at 25% opacity. lens must be one of: show_arch, show_struct, show_mep, show_all.",
    input_schema={
        "type": "object",
        "properties": {
            "model_id": {"type": "string"},
            "view_id": {"type": "string"},
            "lens": {"type": "string", "enum": ["show_arch", "show_struct", "show_mep", "show_all"]},
        },
        "required": ["model_id", "view_id", "lens"],
    },
),
```

### 8. Tests (`app/tests/api/test_dsc_v3_02.py`)

Cover:

- `SetViewLensCmd` updates `defaultLens` on the target view.
- `SetViewLensCmd` raises for unknown view id.
- `element_passes_lens` helper: `show_all` passes everything; `show_struct` passes `struct` elements only.
- Snapshot serialisation: views default `defaultLens` to `"show_all"` when field is absent.
- `set-view-lens` tool descriptor present in `/api/v3/tools`.

---

## Implementation rules

- **No scope creep.** Do not implement LNS-V3-01 UI (status-bar dropdown) here — that is WP-050.
- **Ghost is opacity only, not hidden.** Elements in ghost state remain in the DOM / scene graph. Only opacity changes.
- **Design pillar D8.** Lens is a per-view modifier; changing it does not mutate any element's `discipline` field.
- **No hex literals** in `packages/web/src/**/*.tsx`. Use `var(--draft-witness)` for ghost colour.

---

## Verify gate

```bash
cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
make verify
pnpm exec tsc --noEmit
cd app && python -m pytest tests/api/test_dsc_v3_02.py -v && python -m pytest tests/ -x -q
```

## Commit and push

```bash
        app/bim_ai/elements.py \

        packages/web/src/plan/ \
        packages/web/src/Viewport.tsx \
        packages/cli/cli.mjs \
        app/tests/api/test_dsc_v3_02.py
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(dsc): DSC-V3-02 view discipline tags — defaultLens + SetViewLens + ghost pass

LensMode type + SetViewLensCmd; defaultLens field on all view elements; engine
handler; useLensFilter hook; 25% ghost pass in plan + 3D renderers; set-view-lens
CLI + tool descriptor.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
git push -u origin feat/v3-dsc-v3-02-view-discipline-tags
```

Implement `useLensFilter(viewId)` hook returning `(elem: BimElem) => 'foreground' | 'ghost'`. Used by both renderers.

### 7. Tool descriptor (`app/bim_ai/api/registry.py`)

```python
ToolDescriptor(
    name="set-view-lens",
    description="Set the discipline lens on a view. Elements not matching the lens render at 25% opacity. lens must be one of: show_arch, show_struct, show_mep, show_all.",
    input_schema={
        "type": "object",
        "properties": {
            "model_id": {"type": "string"},
            "view_id": {"type": "string"},
            "lens": {"type": "string", "enum": ["show_arch", "show_struct", "show_mep", "show_all"]},
        },
        "required": ["model_id", "view_id", "lens"],
    },
),
```

### 8. Tests (`app/tests/api/test_dsc_v3_02.py`)

Cover:

- `SetViewLensCmd` updates `defaultLens` on the target view.
- `SetViewLensCmd` raises for unknown view id.
- `element_passes_lens` helper: `show_all` passes everything; `show_struct` passes `struct` elements only.
- Snapshot serialisation: views default `defaultLens` to `"show_all"` when field is absent.
- `set-view-lens` tool descriptor present in `/api/v3/tools`.

---

## Implementation rules

- **No scope creep.** Do not implement LNS-V3-01 UI (status-bar dropdown) here — that is WP-050.
- **Ghost is opacity only, not hidden.** Elements in ghost state remain in the DOM / scene graph. Only opacity changes.
- **Design pillar D8.** Lens is a per-view modifier; changing it does not mutate any element's `discipline` field.
- **No hex literals** in `packages/web/src/**/*.tsx`. Use `var(--draft-witness)` for ghost colour.

---

# WP-051 — CAN-V3-02: Hatch patterns scaling with paper-mm at the active plot scale

**Branch:** `feat/v3-can-v3-02-hatch-patterns`
**Effort:** M — 1.5 weeks
**Status:** `now` (depends on CAN-V3-01 — now `done`)

---

## Required reading

Read every file below end-to-end before writing a single line of code.

- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/workpackage-tracker-v3.md` — §6 T4 CAN-V3-02 entry; §3 Design Pillars D3, D4; §A Agent Instructions
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/CLAUDE.md`
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/viewport/meshBuilders.ts` — existing material + wall rendering; hatch is currently stubbed or absent
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/style/tokens-drafting.css` — drafting token file; add `--hatch-*` tokens here
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/core/src/index.ts` — element types; add `HatchPatternDef` here
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/elements.py` — add `HatchPatternDefElem` if hatches are stored as elements
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/commands.py` — `CreateHatchPatternDefCmd` if needed

---

## Verbatim CAN-V3-02 tracker entry

**Scope.** Canon hatches: brick at 45° offset, concrete dotted, insulation curve-stroked, plaster cross-hatched, timber grain striped, gypsum dotted-finer, stone irregular-poly. Hatches scale with paper-mm at the active plot scale — non-negotiable.

**Data model.** `{ kind: 'hatch_pattern_def'; id; name; paperMmRepeat; rotationDeg; strokeWidthMm; patternKind: 'lines' | 'crosshatch' | 'dots' | 'curve' | 'svg'; svgSource?; }`.

**Engine.** `screenRepeat = paperMmRepeat * (1/scaleDenom) * zoomFactor`. Every wall/floor/detail_region with a hatchId resolves through this.

**UI.** Material editor gains "Plan hatch" picker. Detail-region OptionsBar picks from the same library.

**Acceptance.** 1:50 plan with brick wall; hatch repeats at 73 mm × scale. Switch to 1:100; hatch repeats at half screen density but same brick rhythm in real-world mm. Hatches do NOT rescale on viewport zoom.

**Effort.** M — 1.5 weeks.

---

## Setup

```bash
cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
git fetch origin
git checkout main
git pull --ff-only
git checkout -b feat/v3-can-v3-02-hatch-patterns
uv sync && pnpm install
```

---

## Implementation tasks

### 1. `HatchPatternDefElem` (`app/bim_ai/elements.py`)

```python
class HatchPatternDefElem(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    kind: Literal["hatch_pattern_def"] = "hatch_pattern_def"
    id: str
    name: str
    paper_mm_repeat: float = Field(alias="paperMmRepeat")
    rotation_deg: float = Field(default=0.0, alias="rotationDeg")
    stroke_width_mm: float = Field(default=0.18, alias="strokeWidthMm")
    pattern_kind: Literal["lines", "crosshatch", "dots", "curve", "svg"] = Field(alias="patternKind")
    svg_source: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="svgSource")
```

Add `'hatch_pattern_def'` to `ElemKind` and `HatchPatternDefElem` to `BimElem` in both Python and TypeScript.

### 2. Seed hatch definitions

In the engine initialisation (or a `seed_hatches()` function called at model-create time), add the 7 canon hatches:

```python
SEED_HATCHES = [
    {"id": "brick_45",       "name": "Brick",      "paperMmRepeat": 73,  "rotationDeg": 45,  "strokeWidthMm": 0.18, "patternKind": "lines"},
    {"id": "concrete_dot",   "name": "Concrete",   "paperMmRepeat": 5,   "rotationDeg": 0,   "strokeWidthMm": 0.13, "patternKind": "dots"},
    {"id": "insulation",     "name": "Insulation", "paperMmRepeat": 100, "rotationDeg": 0,   "strokeWidthMm": 0.25, "patternKind": "curve"},
    {"id": "plaster",        "name": "Plaster",    "paperMmRepeat": 8,   "rotationDeg": 45,  "strokeWidthMm": 0.13, "patternKind": "crosshatch"},
    {"id": "timber_grain",   "name": "Timber",     "paperMmRepeat": 12,  "rotationDeg": 0,   "strokeWidthMm": 0.18, "patternKind": "lines"},
    {"id": "gypsum",         "name": "Gypsum",     "paperMmRepeat": 4,   "rotationDeg": 45,  "strokeWidthMm": 0.10, "patternKind": "dots"},
    {"id": "stone",          "name": "Stone",      "paperMmRepeat": 60,  "rotationDeg": 0,   "strokeWidthMm": 0.18, "patternKind": "lines"},
]
```

These are injected into the project as `HatchPatternDefElem` items at model initialisation. They are read-only (built-in library).

### 3. TypeScript types (`packages/core/src/index.ts`)

```ts
export type HatchPatternDef = {
  kind: 'hatch_pattern_def';
  id: string;
  name: string;
  paperMmRepeat: number;
  rotationDeg: number;
  strokeWidthMm: number;
  patternKind: 'lines' | 'crosshatch' | 'dots' | 'curve' | 'svg';
  svgSource?: string | null;
};
```

### 4. Hatch renderer (`packages/web/src/plan/HatchRenderer.ts`)

Core function:

```ts
export function computeHatchScreenRepeat(
  paperMmRepeat: number,
  plotScaleDenominator: number,  // e.g. 50 for 1:50
  viewportZoom: number,          // canvas zoom factor (1 = 100%)
): number {
  // Zoom affects screen pixels but NOT paper scale.
  // At 1:50, 1 paper-mm = 1000/50 = 20 real-mm on paper → scaled to screen pixels by zoom.
  const pixelsPerMm = viewportZoom * (96 / 25.4); // 96 dpi base
  return (paperMmRepeat / plotScaleDenominator) * pixelsPerMm;
}
```

Render functions per `patternKind` using SVG `<pattern>` elements:

---

## Verbatim EDT-V3-06 tracker entry

#### EDT-V3-06 — Helper dimensions on selection (drag-the-number)

**Status.** `now`. D1 in its purest form — the dimension chip IS the input.

**Scope.** On single-element selection, render helper dimension chips at salient measurements. Each chip shows its value. Click the chip → numeric input → type → Enter commits. Tab cycles between chains. The chip IS the input field — no Inspector trip.

**Data model.** `HelperDimensionDescriptor[] = { id, valueMm, label, fromMm, toMm, onCommit: (newValueMm) => Command }`. Reuse `TempDimTarget` rendering.

**Engine.** No engine change. `onCommit` dispatches existing commands.

**Acceptance.** Select a wall — length + thickness chips render. `"5400" + Enter` commits 5.4 m wall. Tab cycles to thickness. Chips update live when geometry moves.

**Effort.** M — 1.5 weeks. Provider extension + render + per-element wiring (wall, door, window, floor, column, beam).

---

## Setup

```bash
cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
git fetch origin
git checkout main
git pull --ff-only
git checkout -b feat/v3-edt-v3-06-helper-dims
uv sync && pnpm install
```

---

## Implementation tasks

### 1. `HelperDimensionDescriptor` type (`packages/core/src/index.ts`)

```ts
export type HelperDimensionDescriptor = {
  id: string;
  label: string;
  valueMm: number;
  /** Start and end points in plan mm coordinates, for drawing the dimension line */
  fromPoint: { x: number; y: number };
  toPoint: { x: number; y: number };
  /** Called when user commits a new value; returns the command to dispatch */
  onCommit: (newValueMm: number) => Record<string, unknown>;
};
```

### 2. `helperDimensions()` providers per element kind

Create `packages/web/src/plan/helperDimensions.ts` exporting a single function:

```ts
export function getHelperDimensions(
  elem: BimElem,
  model: BimModel,
): HelperDimensionDescriptor[] {
  switch (elem.kind) {
    case 'wall': return wallHelperDims(elem, model);
    case 'door': return doorHelperDims(elem, model);
    case 'window': return windowHelperDims(elem, model);
    case 'floor': return floorHelperDims(elem, model);
    case 'column': return columnHelperDims(elem, model);
    case 'beam': return beamHelperDims(elem, model);
    default: return [];
  }
}
```

Per-element helpers:

**Wall:** length (along wall axis from start to end), thickness. `onCommit` for length dispatches `{ type: 'update_wall', id, lengthMm: newValue }` (or equivalent existing command). Thickness dispatches `{ type: 'update_wall', id, thicknessMm: newValue }`.

**Door:** width. `onCommit` dispatches `{ type: 'update_door', id, widthMm: newValue }`.

**Window:** width, sill height, height. Three chips.

**Floor:** area is derived — show it read-only (chip renders but clicking opens nothing). For floors with a `widthMm` / `lengthMm`, those are editable.

**Column:** width, depth. Dispatches `{ type: 'update_column', id, widthMm, depthMm }`.

**Beam:** length (read-only from start/end points, not directly editable). Show for reference.

If an update command for a specific field doesn't exist yet, add a minimal `update_{kind}` command that accepts a partial patch object. Engine handler: look up element, apply patch via `model_copy`.

### 3. `HelperDimChip` component (`packages/web/src/plan/HelperDimChip.tsx`)

```tsx
type HelperDimChipProps = {
  descriptor: HelperDimensionDescriptor;
  planToScreen: (pt: { x: number; y: number }) => { x: number; y: number };
  onDispatch: (cmd: Record<string, unknown>) => void;
};
```

Renders as an SVG overlay on the plan canvas:

- Dimension line between `fromPoint` and `toPoint` in `var(--draft-witness)` stroke at `var(--draft-lw-witness)`
- Chip at the midpoint: `--surface-2` background, `--text-2xs` tabular-nums, `--color-foreground` text
- On click: replace chip with `<foreignObject><input type="number" /></foreignObject>`; value = current mm; Enter commits via `onCommit(parseFloat(input.value))`; Esc cancels; Tab focuses the next chip in the Tab order
- Hover: chip border highlights `var(--brand-accent)`
- **No hex literals.**

### 4. `HelperDimsLayer` component (`packages/web/src/plan/HelperDimsLayer.tsx`)

Renders as an SVG layer above the selection handles when exactly one element is selected:

```tsx
export function HelperDimsLayer({ selectedElemId, model, planToScreen, onDispatch }: { ... }) {
  const elem = model.elements[selectedElemId];
  if (!elem) return null;
  const dims = getHelperDimensions(elem, model);
  return (
    <>
      {dims.map(d => (
        <HelperDimChip
          key={d.id}
          descriptor={d}
          planToScreen={planToScreen}
          onDispatch={onDispatch}
        />
      ))}
    </>
  );
}
```

Wire into the plan canvas selection layer. The layer renders only on single-element selection (not multi-select — chips are meaningless on multiple elements).

### 5. Tab cycling

When a chip's input is focused, Tab moves focus to the next `HelperDimChip` input in DOM order. This is natural browser behaviour if the chips render in document order — verify Tab doesn't escape to the main toolbar.

### 6. Tests (`packages/web/src/plan/__tests__/helperDimensions.test.ts`)

Cover:

- `getHelperDimensions` for a wall returns descriptors with `id: 'wall-length'` and `id: 'wall-thickness'`.
- `onCommit(5400)` on the length descriptor returns the correct update command.
- `getHelperDimensions` for a door returns a descriptor for width.
- Unknown element kind returns `[]`.

No Python tests needed (frontend-only; no engine change).

---

## Implementation rules

- **No engine changes** unless a specific update command is genuinely missing (then add a minimal patch command only).
- **D1 antidote to A2.** Chips show instance values. Type-level edits are an Inspector concern.
- **A9 antidote.** Zero chips visible when nothing is selected — `HelperDimsLayer` renders `null` when `selectedElemId` is null.
- **No hex literals** in any `packages/web/src/**/*.tsx`.

# WP-054 — MAT-V3-01: Material PBR map slots + Decals

**Branch:** `feat/v3-mat-v3-01-pbr-material`
**Effort:** M — 1 week
**Status:** `now` (no deps)

---

## Required reading

Read every file below end-to-end before writing a single line of code.

- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/workpackage-tracker-v3.md` — §7 T5 MAT-V3-01 entry; §3 Design Pillars D3, D4; §A Agent Instructions
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/CLAUDE.md`
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/elements.py` — existing `MaterialElem` (or Material dict); extend here
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/core/src/index.ts` — existing `MaterialElem`; extend + add `DecalElem`
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/viewport/meshBuilders.ts` — 3D mesh renderer; extend material handling
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/commands.py` — add `UpdateMaterialPbrCmd`, `CreateDecalCmd`

---

## Verbatim MAT-V3-01 tracker entry

#### MAT-V3-01 — Material PBR map slots + decals

**Scope.** Extend Material to optional PBR map slots: `albedoMapId`, `normalMapId`, `roughnessMapId`, `metallicMapId`, `heightMapId`. Each map is an ImageAsset referenced by id. Add `Decal` element kind: 2D image hosted to a parent surface with a rectangle of UV space. Materials render in 3D viewport with PBR shader; in plan/elevation, materials render as albedo or hatch. Decals render only in 3D and rendered output.

**Data model.**
```ts
{
  kind: 'material';
  albedoColor?: string;
  albedoMapId?: string;
  normalMapId?: string;
  roughnessMapId?: string;
  metallicMapId?: string;
  heightMapId?: string;
  uvScaleMm?: { uMm: number; vMm: number };
  uvRotationDeg?: number;
  hatchPatternId?: string;
}
{
  kind: 'decal';
  parentElementId: string;
  parentSurface: 'front' | 'back' | 'top';
  imageAssetId: string;
  uvRect: { u0, v0, u1, v1 };
}
```

**Effort.** M — 1 week.

---

## Setup

```bash
cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
git fetch origin
git checkout main
git pull --ff-only
git checkout -b feat/v3-mat-v3-01-pbr-material
uv sync && pnpm install
```

---

## Implementation tasks

### 1. Extend `MaterialElem` in Python (`app/bim_ai/elements.py`)

Add optional PBR fields to the existing `Material` class (whatever it's currently called):

```python
albedo_map_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="albedoMapId")
normal_map_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="normalMapId")
roughness_map_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="roughnessMapId")
metallic_map_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="metallicMapId")
height_map_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="heightMapId")
uv_scale_mm: dict | None = Field(default=None, alias="uvScaleMm")  # {uMm, vMm}
uv_rotation_deg: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="uvRotationDeg")
hatch_pattern_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="hatchPatternId")
```

### 2. `DecalElem` (`app/bim_ai/elements.py`)

```python
class DecalElem(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    kind: Literal["decal"] = "decal"
    id: str
    parent_element_id: str = Field(alias="parentElementId")
    parent_surface: Literal["front", "back", "top", "left", "right", "bottom"] = Field(alias="parentSurface")
    image_asset_id: str = Field(alias="imageAssetId")
    uv_rect: dict = Field(alias="uvRect")  # {u0, v0, u1, v1}
    opacity: float = 1.0
```

Add `'decal'` to `ElemKind` and `DecalElem` to `BimElem` in Python and TypeScript.

### 3. Commands (`app/bim_ai/commands.py`)

```python
class UpdateMaterialPbrCmd(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["update_material_pbr"] = "update_material_pbr"
    id: str
    albedo_map_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="albedoMapId")
    normal_map_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="normalMapId")
    roughness_map_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="roughnessMapId")
    metallic_map_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="metallicMapId")
    height_map_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="heightMapId")
    uv_scale_mm: dict | None = Field(default=None, alias="uvScaleMm")
    uv_rotation_deg: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="uvRotationDeg")
    hatch_pattern_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="hatchPatternId")

class CreateDecalCmd(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["create_decal"] = "create_decal"
    id: str
    parent_element_id: str = Field(alias="parentElementId")
    parent_surface: str = Field(alias="parentSurface")
    image_asset_id: str = Field(alias="imageAssetId")
    uv_rect: dict = Field(alias="uvRect")
    opacity: float = 1.0
```

Add both to the `Command` union. Add engine handlers in `apply_inplace`.

### 4. TypeScript types (`packages/core/src/index.ts`)

Extend `MaterialElem` with the PBR fields. Add:

```ts
export type DecalElem = {
  kind: 'decal';
  id: string;
  parentElementId: string;
  parentSurface: 'front' | 'back' | 'top' | 'left' | 'right' | 'bottom';
  imageAssetId: string;
  uvRect: { u0: number; v0: number; u1: number; v1: number };
  opacity?: number;
};
```

### 5. 3D viewport PBR rendering (`packages/web/src/viewport/meshBuilders.ts`)

When building a mesh for a wall/floor/element that has a material with PBR map slots:

- If `albedoMapId` is set, fetch the image asset by id from the model's `imageAssets` store and apply as the albedo texture
- If `normalMapId` is set, apply as normal map
- For `roughnessMapId` and `metallicMapId`, apply to the PBR material's roughness/metallic channels
- UV scaling: apply `uvScaleMm` to control the texture repeat. Default: `{ uMm: 1000, vMm: 1000 }` (1 m repeat).
- For v3, a simplified PBR shader is sufficient: albedo texture + normal map + roughness scalar. Full PBR will be refined in T5.

For `DecalElem`: render as a projected plane in 3D at the specified `uvRect` on the parent surface. Use a `MeshBasicMaterial` with the image asset texture and `transparent: true`.

### 6. Tool descriptor (`app/bim_ai/api/registry.py`)

```python
ToolDescriptor(
    name="update-material-pbr",
    description="Set PBR map slots on a material element. Each map is an image asset id. Pass null to clear a slot.",
    input_schema={
        "type": "object",
        "properties": {
            "model_id": {"type": "string"},
            "id": {"type": "string", "description": "Material element id"},
            "albedoMapId": {"type": "string"},
            "normalMapId": {"type": "string"},
            "roughnessMapId": {"type": "string"},
            "metallicMapId": {"type": "string"},
            "uvScaleMm": {"type": "object", "properties": {"uMm": {"type": "number"}, "vMm": {"type": "number"}}},
        },
        "required": ["model_id", "id"],
    },
),
```

### 7. Tests (`app/tests/api/test_mat_v3_01.py`)

Cover:

- `UpdateMaterialPbrCmd` patches `albedoMapId` and `normalMapId` on a material element.
- `CreateDecalCmd` stores a `DecalElem` with correct parentElementId, surface, uvRect.
- `update-material-pbr` tool descriptor present in `/api/v3/tools`.
- Engine raises for unknown material id.
- Round-trip: create decal → fetch snapshot → decal present with correct kind.

---

## Implementation rules

- **No scope creep.** Do not implement AST-V3-05 (curved-surface UV handles) or MAT-V3-02 (token migration) here.
- **Plan view.** Materials render as `albedoColor` or hatch in plan/elevation — no PBR in 2D. PBR is 3D only.
- **No hex literals** in `packages/web/src/**/*.tsx`.

---

## Verify gate

```bash
cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
make verify
pnpm exec tsc --noEmit
cd app && python -m pytest tests/api/test_mat_v3_01.py -v && python -m pytest tests/ -x -q
```

---

## Commit and push

```bash
git add app/bim_ai/elements.py \
        app/bim_ai/commands.py \
        app/bim_ai/engine.py \
        app/bim_ai/api/registry.py \
        packages/core/src/index.ts \
        packages/web/src/viewport/meshBuilders.ts \
        app/tests/api/test_mat_v3_01.py
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
```

## Verbatim MAT-V3-01 tracker entry

#### MAT-V3-01 — Material PBR map slots + decals

**Scope.** Extend Material to optional PBR map slots: `albedoMapId`, `normalMapId`, `roughnessMapId`, `metallicMapId`, `heightMapId`. Each map is an ImageAsset referenced by id. Add `Decal` element kind: 2D image hosted to a parent surface with a rectangle of UV space. Materials render in 3D viewport with PBR shader; in plan/elevation, materials render as albedo or hatch. Decals render only in 3D and rendered output.

**Data model.**
```ts
{
  kind: 'material';
  albedoColor?: string;
  albedoMapId?: string;
  normalMapId?: string;
  roughnessMapId?: string;
  metallicMapId?: string;
  heightMapId?: string;
  uvScaleMm?: { uMm: number; vMm: number };
  uvRotationDeg?: number;
  hatchPatternId?: string;
}
{
  kind: 'decal';
  parentElementId: string;
  parentSurface: 'front' | 'back' | 'top';
  imageAssetId: string;
  uvRect: { u0, v0, u1, v1 };
}
```

**Effort.** M — 1 week.

---

```python
normal_map_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="normalMapId")
roughness_map_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="roughnessMapId")
metallic_map_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="metallicMapId")
```

### 3. Commands (`app/bim_ai/commands.py`)

```python
class UpdateMaterialPbrCmd(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    type: Literal["update_material_pbr"] = "update_material_pbr"
    id: str
    albedo_map_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="albedoMapId")

```

Add both to the `Command` union. Add engine handlers in `apply_inplace`.

### 4. TypeScript types (`packages/core/src/index.ts`)

Extend `MaterialElem` with the PBR fields. Add:

```ts
export type DecalElem = {
  kind: 'decal';
  id: string;
  parentElementId: string;
  parentSurface: 'front' | 'back' | 'top' | 'left' | 'right' | 'bottom';
  imageAssetId: string;
  uvRect: { u0: number; v0: number; u1: number; v1: number };
  opacity?: number;
};
```

### 5. 3D viewport PBR rendering (`packages/web/src/viewport/meshBuilders.ts`)

When building a mesh for a wall/floor/element that has a material with PBR map slots:

- If `albedoMapId` is set, fetch the image asset by id from the model's `imageAssets` store and apply as the albedo texture
- If `normalMapId` is set, apply as normal map
- For `roughnessMapId` and `metallicMapId`, apply to the PBR material's roughness/metallic channels
- UV scaling: apply `uvScaleMm` to control the texture repeat. Default: `{ uMm: 1000, vMm: 1000 }` (1 m repeat).
- For v3, a simplified PBR shader is sufficient: albedo texture + normal map + roughness scalar. Full PBR will be refined in T5.

For `DecalElem`: render as a projected plane in 3D at the specified `uvRect` on the parent surface. Use a `MeshBasicMaterial` with the image asset texture and `transparent: true`.

### 6. Tool descriptor (`app/bim_ai/api/registry.py`)

```python
ToolDescriptor(
    name="update-material-pbr",
    description="Set PBR map slots on a material element. Each map is an image asset id. Pass null to clear a slot.",

        "type": "object",
        "properties": {
            "model_id": {"type": "string"},
            "id": {"type": "string", "description": "Material element id"},
            "albedoMapId": {"type": "string"},
            "normalMapId": {"type": "string"},
            "roughnessMapId": {"type": "string"},
            "metallicMapId": {"type": "string"},
            "uvScaleMm": {"type": "object", "properties": {"uMm": {"type": "number"}, "vMm": {"type": "number"}}},
        },
        "required": ["model_id", "id"],
    },
),
```

Cover:

- `UpdateMaterialPbrCmd` patches `albedoMapId` and `normalMapId` on a material element.
- `CreateDecalCmd` stores a `DecalElem` with correct parentElementId, surface, uvRect.
- `update-material-pbr` tool descriptor present in `/api/v3/tools`.
- Engine raises for unknown material id.
- Round-trip: create decal → fetch snapshot → decal present with correct kind.

**Branch:** `feat/v3-sch-v3-01-schedule-view`
**Effort:** M — 1.5 weeks
**Status:** `now` (no deps)

---

## Required reading

Read every file below end-to-end before writing a single line of code.

- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/workpackage-tracker-v3.md` — §9 T9 SCH-V3-01 entry; §3 Design Pillars D1, D8; §A Agent Instructions
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/CLAUDE.md`
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/elements.py` — existing element classes; understand what fields are kernel-derived
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/commands.py` — existing command union
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/routes_api.py` — add schedule query endpoint here
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/core/src/index.ts` — element types; add `PropertyDefinition`, `ScheduleViewElem`

---

## Verbatim SCH-V3-01 tracker entry

#### SCH-V3-01 — Custom-properties + filterable spreadsheet view

**Scope.** Two coupled primitives. **(a)** Custom-properties: `PropertyDefinition` per project; element-level `props: Record<string, unknown>`. **(b)** Schedule view: filterable spreadsheet over a category; columns = built-in fields + custom-properties; rows = element instances. Filter/sort/group. Edit any cell → mutates element via command bus. Cross-highlight: hover row → element pulses in plan/3D.

**Data model.**
```ts
type PropertyDefinition = {
  key: string; label: string;
  kind: 'mm' | 'm2' | 'currency' | 'enum' | 'string' | 'bool' | 'date';
  enumValues?: string[];
  defaultValue?: unknown;
  appliesTo: Array<ElemKind | '*'>;
  showInSchedule: boolean;
};
type ScheduleView = {
  kind: 'schedule'; id: string; name: string;
  category: ElemKind;
  columns: Array<{ fieldKey: string; label: string; width?: number }>;
  filterExpr?: string;
  sortKey?: string; sortDir?: 'asc' | 'desc';
};
```

**Effort.** M — 1.5 weeks.

---

## Setup

```bash
cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
git fetch origin
git checkout main
git pull --ff-only
git checkout -b feat/v3-sch-v3-01-schedule-view
uv sync && pnpm install
```

---

## Implementation tasks

### 1. `PropertyDefinition` element (`app/bim_ai/elements.py`)

```python
class PropertyDefinitionElem(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    kind: Literal["property_definition"] = "property_definition"
    id: str
    key: str
    label: str
    prop_kind: Literal["mm", "m2", "currency", "enum", "string", "bool", "date"] = Field(alias="propKind")
    enum_values: list[str] | None = Field(default=None, alias="enumValues")
    default_value: Any | None = Field(default=None, alias="defaultValue")
    applies_to: list[str] = Field(alias="appliesTo")  # ElemKind values or '*'
    show_in_schedule: bool = Field(default=True, alias="showInSchedule")
```

Add `'property_definition'` to `ElemKind`.

### 2. `props` field on all element types

All existing element classes gain an optional `props` field:

```python
props: dict[str, Any] | None = Field(default=None)
```

This is additive — existing elements without `props` default to `None` (treated as `{}`).

### 3. `ScheduleViewElem` (`app/bim_ai/elements.py`)

```python
class ScheduleViewElem(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    kind: Literal["schedule"] = "schedule"
    id: str
    name: str
    category: str  # ElemKind value
    columns: list[dict] = Field(default_factory=list)  # [{fieldKey, label, width?}]
    filter_expr: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="filterExpr")
    sort_key: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="sortKey")
    sort_dir: Literal["asc", "desc"] | None = Field(default=None, alias="sortDir")
```

Add `'schedule'` to `ElemKind`.

### 4. Commands (`app/bim_ai/commands.py`)

```python
class CreatePropertyDefinitionCmd(BaseModel):
    type: Literal["create_property_definition"] = "create_property_definition"
    id: str
    key: str
    label: str
    prop_kind: str = Field(alias="propKind")
    enum_values: list[str] | None = Field(default=None, alias="enumValues")
    default_value: Any | None = Field(default=None, alias="defaultValue")
    applies_to: list[str] = Field(alias="appliesTo")
    show_in_schedule: bool = Field(default=True, alias="showInSchedule")

class SetElementPropCmd(BaseModel):
    type: Literal["set_element_prop"] = "set_element_prop"
    element_id: str = Field(alias="elementId")
    key: str
    value: Any

class CreateScheduleViewCmd(BaseModel):
    type: Literal["create_schedule_view"] = "create_schedule_view"
    id: str
    name: str
    category: str
    columns: list[dict] = Field(default_factory=list)
```

Add all three to the `Command` union. Engine handlers:

- `CreatePropertyDefinitionCmd` → stores `PropertyDefinitionElem`
- `SetElementPropCmd` → finds element by `element_id`, updates `props[key] = value` via `model_copy`
- `CreateScheduleViewCmd` → stores `ScheduleViewElem`

### 5. Schedule query endpoint (`app/bim_ai/routes_api.py`)

```
GET /api/v3/models/{modelId}/schedules/{scheduleId}/rows
```

Returns a JSON array of rows, one per element matching `scheduleView.category`. Each row:
```json
{
  "elementId": "...",
  "fields": {
    "id": "...",
    "name": "...",
    "lengthMm": 4200,
    "cost_per_m2": 85.0
  }
}
```

Built-in fields are derived from the element (e.g. `lengthMm` for walls, `widthMm` for doors). Custom-property fields come from `element.props`. Apply `filterExpr` (simple string match on any field value), `sortKey` / `sortDir`.

Requires `viewer` permission.

### 6. TypeScript types (`packages/core/src/index.ts`)

```ts
export type PropertyDefinitionElem = {
  kind: 'property_definition';
  id: string;
  key: string;
  label: string;
  propKind: 'mm' | 'm2' | 'currency' | 'enum' | 'string' | 'bool' | 'date';
  enumValues?: string[];
  defaultValue?: unknown;
  appliesTo: string[];
  showInSchedule: boolean;
};

export type ScheduleViewElem = {
  kind: 'schedule';
  id: string;
  name: string;
  category: string;
  columns: Array<{ fieldKey: string; label: string; width?: number }>;
  filterExpr?: string | null;
  sortKey?: string | null;
  sortDir?: 'asc' | 'desc' | null;
};
```

Add `props?: Record<string, unknown>` to all `BimElem` variants.

### 7. `ScheduleView` component (`packages/web/src/workspace/ScheduleView.tsx`)

Spreadsheet-style table component:

- Fetches rows from `GET /schedules/{id}/rows` on mount + on model update
- Renders `<table>` with sticky header row showing column labels
- Each data row: cell per column, renders value in `--text-sm` tabular-nums
- Click any editable cell → inline `<input>` → Enter dispatches `SetElementPropCmd` or the relevant update command for built-in fields
- Hover a row → dispatches a `HIGHLIGHT_ELEMENT` UI action (element pulses in plan canvas for 1.5 s)
- Filter bar at top: text input → re-fetches with `filterExpr`
- Sort: click column header → toggles asc/desc
- No hex literals. All colours via `var(--*)` tokens.

### 8. Activate from Project Browser / View menu

The schedule view appears in the project browser under "Schedules." Double-click → opens in the main canvas area as a tab. Alternatively, wire to a "New Schedule" menu item in the View menu.

### 9. Tests (`app/tests/api/test_sch_v3_01.py`)

Cover:

- `CreatePropertyDefinitionCmd` stores a `PropertyDefinitionElem`.
- `SetElementPropCmd` sets `props.cost_per_m2 = 85.0` on a wall element.
- `GET /schedules/{id}/rows` returns correct rows for a schedule with category `wall`.
- Filter by `filterExpr` narrows rows correctly.
- `SetElementPropCmd` for unknown element raises.
- Custom property value round-trips through snapshot.
- `create-schedule-view` + `set-element-prop` tool descriptors present in `/api/v3/tools`.

---

## Implementation rules

- **No scope creep.** Do not implement SCH-V3-02 (schedule export to layout page).
- **`filterExpr` is simple string match for v3.** No DSL. Full filter DSL is future.
- **No hex literals** in `packages/web/src/**/*.tsx`.
- **D1 antidote.** Cell edit dispatches a real command through the standard bus — no special "schedule mode."

---

## Verify gate

```bash
make verify
pnpm exec tsc --noEmit
cd app && python -m pytest tests/api/test_sch_v3_01.py -v && python -m pytest tests/ -x -q
```

```bash
        packages/core/src/index.ts \

        app/tests/api/test_sch_v3_01.py

feat(sch): SCH-V3-01 custom-properties + filterable schedule view

PropertyDefinitionElem + props field on all elements; SetElementPropCmd;
ScheduleViewElem + CreateScheduleViewCmd; GET /schedules/{id}/rows endpoint;
ScheduleView spreadsheet component with inline cell edit + cross-highlight.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
```

```python
    filter_expr: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="filterExpr")
    sort_key: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="sortKey")
    sort_dir: Literal["asc", "desc"] | None = Field(default=None, alias="sortDir")
```

Add `'schedule'` to `ElemKind`.

### 4. Commands (`app/bim_ai/commands.py`)

```python
class CreatePropertyDefinitionCmd(BaseModel):
    type: Literal["create_property_definition"] = "create_property_definition"
    id: str
    key: str
    label: str
    prop_kind: str = Field(alias="propKind")
    enum_values: list[str] | None = Field(default=None, alias="enumValues")
    default_value: Any | None = Field(default=None, alias="defaultValue")
    applies_to: list[str] = Field(alias="appliesTo")
    show_in_schedule: bool = Field(default=True, alias="showInSchedule")

class SetElementPropCmd(BaseModel):
    type: Literal["set_element_prop"] = "set_element_prop"
    element_id: str = Field(alias="elementId")
    key: str
    value: Any

class CreateScheduleViewCmd(BaseModel):
    type: Literal["create_schedule_view"] = "create_schedule_view"
    id: str
    name: str
    category: str
    columns: list[dict] = Field(default_factory=list)
```

Add all three to the `Command` union. Engine handlers:

- `CreatePropertyDefinitionCmd` → stores `PropertyDefinitionElem`
- `SetElementPropCmd` → finds element by `element_id`, updates `props[key] = value` via `model_copy`
- `CreateScheduleViewCmd` → stores `ScheduleViewElem`

### 5. Schedule query endpoint (`app/bim_ai/routes_api.py`)

```
GET /api/v3/models/{modelId}/schedules/{scheduleId}/rows
```

Returns a JSON array of rows, one per element matching `scheduleView.category`. Each row:
```json
{
  "elementId": "...",
  "fields": {
    "id": "...",
    "name": "...",
    "lengthMm": 4200,
    "cost_per_m2": 85.0
  }
}
```

Built-in fields are derived from the element (e.g. `lengthMm` for walls, `widthMm` for doors). Custom-property fields come from `element.props`. Apply `filterExpr` (simple string match on any field value), `sortKey` / `sortDir`.

Requires `viewer` permission.

### 6. TypeScript types (`packages/core/src/index.ts`)

```ts
export type PropertyDefinitionElem = {
  kind: 'property_definition';
  id: string;
  key: string;
  label: string;
  propKind: 'mm' | 'm2' | 'currency' | 'enum' | 'string' | 'bool' | 'date';
  enumValues?: string[];
  defaultValue?: unknown;
  appliesTo: string[];
  showInSchedule: boolean;
};

export type ScheduleViewElem = {
  kind: 'schedule';
  id: string;
  name: string;
  category: string;
  columns: Array<{ fieldKey: string; label: string; width?: number }>;
  filterExpr?: string | null;
  sortKey?: string | null;
  sortDir?: 'asc' | 'desc' | null;
};
```

Add `props?: Record<string, unknown>` to all `BimElem` variants.

### 7. `ScheduleView` component (`packages/web/src/workspace/ScheduleView.tsx`)

Spreadsheet-style table component:

- Fetches rows from `GET /schedules/{id}/rows` on mount + on model update
- Renders `<table>` with sticky header row showing column labels
- Each data row: cell per column, renders value in `--text-sm` tabular-nums
- Click any editable cell → inline `<input>` → Enter dispatches `SetElementPropCmd` or the relevant update command for built-in fields
- Hover a row → dispatches a `HIGHLIGHT_ELEMENT` UI action (element pulses in plan canvas for 1.5 s)
- Filter bar at top: text input → re-fetches with `filterExpr`
- Sort: click column header → toggles asc/desc
- No hex literals. All colours via `var(--*)` tokens.

### 8. Activate from Project Browser / View menu

The schedule view appears in the project browser under "Schedules." Double-click → opens in the main canvas area as a tab. Alternatively, wire to a "New Schedule" menu item in the View menu.

### 9. Tests (`app/tests/api/test_sch_v3_01.py`)

Cover:

- `CreatePropertyDefinitionCmd` stores a `PropertyDefinitionElem`.
- `SetElementPropCmd` sets `props.cost_per_m2 = 85.0` on a wall element.
- `GET /schedules/{id}/rows` returns correct rows for a schedule with category `wall`.
- Filter by `filterExpr` narrows rows correctly.
- `SetElementPropCmd` for unknown element raises.
- Custom property value round-trips through snapshot.
- `create-schedule-view` + `set-element-prop` tool descriptors present in `/api/v3/tools`.

---

## Implementation rules

- **No scope creep.** Do not implement SCH-V3-02 (schedule export to layout page).
- **`filterExpr` is simple string match for v3.** No DSL. Full filter DSL is future.
- **No hex literals** in `packages/web/src/**/*.tsx`.
- **D1 antidote.** Cell edit dispatches a real command through the standard bus — no special "schedule mode."

---

## Verify gate

```bash
cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
make verify
pnpm exec tsc --noEmit
cd app && python -m pytest tests/api/test_sch_v3_01.py -v && python -m pytest tests/ -x -q
```

---

## Commit and push

```bash
git add app/bim_ai/elements.py \
        app/bim_ai/commands.py \
        app/bim_ai/engine.py \
        app/bim_ai/routes_api.py \
        app/bim_ai/api/registry.py \
        packages/core/src/index.ts \
        packages/web/src/workspace/ScheduleView.tsx \
        app/tests/api/test_sch_v3_01.py
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(sch): SCH-V3-01 custom-properties + filterable schedule view

PropertyDefinitionElem + props field on all elements; SetElementPropCmd;
ScheduleViewElem + CreateScheduleViewCmd; GET /schedules/{id}/rows endpoint;
ScheduleView spreadsheet component with inline cell edit + cross-highlight.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
git push -u origin feat/v3-sch-v3-01-schedule-view
```

Do NOT open a PR and do NOT merge to main.

---

## Final report template

```
WP: SCH-V3-01
Prompt file: spec/v3-prompts/wp-055.md
Branch: feat/v3-sch-v3-01-schedule-view
Final commit: <full SHA>
Verify: green | red — <reason if red>
Acceptance: yes | partial — <gap if partial> | no
Files touched: <count>
Lines: +<add> -<del>
As-shipped notes: <2-3 sentences>
Follow-up surfaced: <list or none>
```

All done. Here's the full picture:

**index.ts bug fixed** — `PlacedAssetElem` missing closing `};` + duplicate TOP types + 15 duplicate `discipline?` fields. All cleaned up, TypeScript passes clean.

**Tracker updated** — 38 WPs flipped from `now` to `done` to reflect waves 1-6.

**Wave-7 dispatched** — 8 prompt files written (WP-048 to WP-055):

- WP-048 ANN-V3-01 — detail-region drawing tool (B1 critical path)
- WP-049 DSC-V3-02 — `defaultLens` + `SetViewLensCmd` + ghost rendering pass
- WP-050 LNS-V3-01 — status-bar lens dropdown (merges after WP-049)
- WP-051 CAN-V3-02 — hatch patterns scaling with paper-mm
- WP-052 EDT-V3-04 — shortcut chips always visible on toolbar icons
- WP-053 EDT-V3-06 — helper dimension chips on selection (drag-the-number)
- WP-054 MAT-V3-01 — PBR material map slots + decal element
- WP-055 SCH-V3-01 — custom-properties + filterable schedule view

You can now dispatch the 8 agents via `/ultrareview` or however you spin them up. Note: WP-050 (LNS) should be merged after WP-049 (DSC) — the build state notes this.
